text: Setting clear project goals is essential for success, and the SMART framework helps make them effective. SMART stands for Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. Using this method ensures everyone knows exactly what the team is working toward. It is also important to break the project into milestones—smaller, manageable steps. Each milestone helps track progress and keeps motivation high. Deliverables, or tangible results, are linked to these milestones. For example, a completed report or a working prototype can be a deliverable. Finally, stakeholder alignment is key. All people involved—such as clients, managers, or team members—must agree on the goals, milestones, and deliverables. This prevents confusion and keeps the project on track from start to finish.
